title: Emergency Response
learning_objectives: Understand different types of workplace emergencies.
Learn the standard emergency response procedure.
Recognize emergency equipment and evacuation routes.
Learn basic first aid and incident reporting procedures.

introduction.title: What is Emergency Response?
introduction.content: Emergency Response refers to the immediate actions taken to protect people, property and the environment during unexpected situations such as fires, chemical spills, electrical accidents, medical emergencies or natural disasters. A quick, organized and well-practiced response helps minimize injuries and damage.

business_purpose.title: Why is Emergency Response Important?
business_purpose.content: A well-defined emergency response system protects employees, reduces operational losses and ensures business continuity. Regular training, emergency drills and clear communication help employees respond confidently during emergencies.

types_of_emergencies.description: Fire caused by electrical faults, flammable materials or industrial processes.
Injuries, illness or accidents requiring immediate medical attention.
Accidental release of hazardous chemicals or gases.
Electric shock, short circuit or electrical fire.
Unexpected breakdown of critical machinery creating unsafe conditions.
Earthquake, flood, storm or other natural events affecting plant operations.

emergency_response_steps: Remain calm and assess the situation.
Inform the supervisor or emergency control room.
Move to the designated assembly point.
Do not use elevators during emergencies.
Wait for further instructions from emergency responders.

emergency_equipment.purpose: Used to control small fires during the initial stage.
Alerts all personnel about an emergency.
Provides immediate treatment for minor injuries.
Used after exposure to hazardous chemicals.
Contains materials for controlling chemical spills.

employee_responsibilities: Assist others only when it is safe.
first_aid_guidelines: Ensure the area is safe before assisting.
Do not move seriously injured persons unless necessary.
Control bleeding using clean dressings if trained.
Seek immediate medical assistance for serious injuries.
fire_safety_actions: Use the correct type of fire extinguisher only if trained.
Evacuate immediately if the fire cannot be controlled.
chemical_spill_response: Avoid direct contact with spilled chemicals.
Use approved spill kits only if trained.
evacuation_procedure: Leave equipment in a safe condition if time permits.
Proceed calmly to the assembly point.
Wait for official clearance before re-entering.

integration.purpose: Safely shuts down production activities during emergencies.
Coordinates emergency communication and employee accountability.
Controls site access and supports evacuation.
Responds to chemical spills and environmental incidents.

business_example.title: Emergency Response at JSL
business_example.content: During routine production, a hydraulic oil leak catches fire near a rolling mill. The operator immediately activates the fire alarm, informs the control room and safely shuts down nearby equipment. Trained emergency responders use the correct fire extinguisher while employees evacuate to the designated assembly point. After the fire is extinguished, maintenance inspects the equipment before production resumes.

best_practices: Report hazards before they become emergencies.
important_notes: Personal safety is always the highest priority.
Only trained personnel should respond to specialized emergencies.
Never attempt heroic actions that place yourself or others at risk.
Regular emergency drills improve preparedness and response time.
